
```{r}

library(tidyr)
library(reshape2)
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)

sampling_freq <- 2
end_psg <- 1000

data <- read.csv("/Users/alimos313/Documents/studies/phd/hpc-research/genome-evo-proj/src/analysis/neutral-sim/final.0.csv", header = T)

i <- 1
fixed_count_list <- list()
fixed_count <- c()
i <- i + 1
for (psg in seq(2,end_psg,sampling_freq)){
    
    print(psg)

    print("#######")
    data <- read.csv(paste0("/Users/alimos313/Documents/studies/phd/hpc-research/genome-evo-proj/src/analysis/neutral-sim/samples/sample_",psg,"_0.csv"), header = T)

    tab <- table(unlist(strsplit(rep(data$haplotype, data$count), ";")))
    tab <- tab[names(tab) != "wt"]
    counts <- as.numeric(tab)
    pos <- as.numeric(sapply(strsplit(names(tab), ":"), "[[", 1)) + 1
    ref <- sapply(strsplit(sapply(strsplit(names(tab), ":"), "[[", 2), "->"), "[[", 1)
    alt <- sapply(strsplit(sapply(strsplit(names(tab), ":"), "[[", 2), "->"), "[[", 2)


    df <- data.frame(Position = pos, Ref = ref, Alt = alt, Count = counts)
    df <- df[order(df$Position),]

    fixed_count <- append(fixed_count, sum((df$Count/sum(data$count)) == 1))
    print(sum((df$Count/sum(data$count)) == 1))
}

fixed_count_list[[i]] <- fixed_count

# Convert list to data frame
fixed_count_df <- as.data.frame(fixed_count_list)

# Rename columns to run_1, run_2, ..., run_7
colnames(fixed_count_df) <- paste0("run_", seq_along(fixed_count_list))
head(fixed_count_df)


# Add time column (row index)
fixed_count_df$time <- seq_len(nrow(fixed_count_df))

# Reshape data from wide to long format
fixed_count_df_long <- melt(fixed_count_df, id.vars = "time", variable.name = "run", value.name = "value")

# Plot using ggplot2
ggplot(fixed_count_df_long, aes(x = time, y = value, group = run, color = run)) +
  geom_line(size = 1, alpha = 0.5) +  # Line plot
  geom_point(size = 2) + # Add points for visibility
  labs(title = "Fixed Variant Count Over Time",
       x = "Time",
       y = "Value",
       color = "Run") +
  theme_minimal()


# size of sampled set
sum(data$count)
# number of haplotypes in the sampled set
nrow(data)
# number of unique mutations in the sampled set
nrow(df)
# highest allele frequency of a variant in the sampled set
max(df$Count/sum(data$count))
# allele frequency distribution of variants in the sampled set
hist(df$Count/sum(data$count))


```
